briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — aggressive winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, they'll bite the Ned Rig on the fall. Cast out, let it sink on dead slack line — don't tighten up until it hits bottom.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: In clear water, they'll bite the Ned Rig on the fall. Cast out, let it sink on dead slack line — don't tighten up until it hits bottom.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Texas rig a Ned bait on a swing head jig to fish snaggy areas where an exposed hook empties your terminal box.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, creek channel, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: In clear deep lakes with shad kills, fish often suspend. The higher they are in the water column, the more active they are. Fish off main lake points, humps, drops, centers of big bays — not near the bank. Alabama rig with spinners (keeps rig...

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
